import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import math
import torch.nn.functional as F


class DKT(nn.Module):
    """LSTM-based Deep Knowledge Tracing with learned embeddings"""
    def __init__(self, num_skills, embed_dim=128, hidden_dim=256, num_layers=2, dropout=0.3):
        super().__init__()
        self.num_skills = num_skills
        # Learned interaction embeddings: 2*num_skills (skill + correct/wrong)
        self.interaction_embed = nn.Embedding(2 * num_skills + 1, embed_dim, padding_idx=2 * num_skills)
        self.lstm = nn.LSTM(
            input_size=embed_dim,
            hidden_size=hidden_dim,
            num_layers=num_layers,
            batch_first=True,
            dropout=dropout if num_layers > 1 else 0.0
        )
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout)
        self.fc = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, num_skills)

    def forward(self, skill_ids, corrects, lengths):
        B, T = skill_ids.shape
        # interaction_ids: skill + correct*num_skills
        interaction_ids = skill_ids + corrects * self.num_skills  # [B, T]
        x = self.interaction_embed(interaction_ids)  # [B, T, embed_dim]
        packed = nn.utils.rnn.pack_padded_sequence(
            x, lengths.cpu(), batch_first=True, enforce_sorted=False
        )
        out, _ = self.lstm(packed)
        out, _ = nn.utils.rnn.pad_packed_sequence(out, batch_first=True, total_length=T)
        out = self.dropout(out)
        logits = self.fc(out)  # [B, T, num_skills]
        return logits


class TransformerKT(nn.Module):
    """
    Transformer-based Knowledge Tracing.
    Uses causal (autoregressive) attention.
    At position t, predicts correctness for skill_ids[t+1] using history 0..t.
    """
    def __init__(self, num_skills, embed_dim=256, num_heads=8, num_layers=4, 
                 dropout=0.2, max_seq_len=512, ff_dim=None):
        super().__init__()
        self.num_skills = num_skills
        self.embed_dim = embed_dim
        if ff_dim is None:
            ff_dim = embed_dim * 4
        
        # Interaction embedding: skill_id + correct/wrong
        self.interaction_embed = nn.Embedding(2 * num_skills + 1, embed_dim, padding_idx=2 * num_skills)
        # Skill embedding for the query (what skill to predict)
        self.skill_embed = nn.Embedding(num_skills + 1, embed_dim, padding_idx=num_skills)
        # Positional encoding
        self.pos_embed = nn.Embedding(max_seq_len + 2, embed_dim)
        
        # Transformer encoder layers (causal)
        encoder_layer = nn.TransformerEncoderLayer(
            d_model=embed_dim,
            nhead=num_heads,
            dim_feedforward=ff_dim,
            dropout=dropout,
            batch_first=True,
            norm_first=True  # Pre-norm for stability
        )
        self.transformer = nn.TransformerEncoder(encoder_layer, num_layers=num_layers)
        
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout)
        self.norm = nn.LayerNorm(embed_dim)
        self.fc = nn.Linear(embed_dim, 1)
        
        self._init_weights()
    
    def _init_weights(self):
        for p in self.parameters():
            if p.dim() > 1:
                nn.init.xavier_uniform_(p)
    
    def forward(self, skill_ids, corrects, lengths):
        """
        Returns logits [B, T-1] for predicting corrects[:, 1:]
        """
        B, T = skill_ids.shape
        device = skill_ids.device
        
        # Input: interactions at positions 0..T-2
        # We want to predict correctness at positions 1..T-1
        # So input sequence length is T-1
        
        # Interaction embeddings for history (positions 0..T-2)
        interaction_ids = skill_ids[:, :-1] + corrects[:, :-1] * self.num_skills  # [B, T-1]
        positions = torch.arange(T-1, device=device).unsqueeze(0)  # [1, T-1]
        
        x = self.interaction_embed(interaction_ids)  # [B, T-1, D]
        x = x + self.pos_embed(positions)  # [B, T-1, D]
        x = self.dropout(x)
        
        # Causal mask: position i can only attend to positions 0..i
        T1 = T - 1
        causal_mask = torch.triu(torch.ones(T1, T1, device=device), diagonal=1).bool()
        
        # Key padding mask: True = ignore
        # For sequence of length L, valid positions are 0..L-2 (for predicting 1..L-1)
        # So valid history positions: 0..L-2, i.e., L-1 positions
        key_padding_mask = torch.arange(T1, device=device).unsqueeze(0) >= (lengths.unsqueeze(1) - 1)  # [B, T-1]
        
        # Run transformer
        out = self.transformer(x, mask=causal_mask, src_key_padding_mask=key_padding_mask)  # [B, T-1, D]
        out = self.norm(out)
        
        # Predict correctness for the NEXT skill
        # At position t in output, we predict skill_ids[:, t+1]
        # But we want to condition on the skill being asked
        # Add skill embedding of the skill to predict
        next_skill_embed = self.skill_embed(skill_ids[:, 1:])  # [B, T-1, D]
        
        # Combine transformer output with next skill embedding
        combined = out + next_skill_embed  # [B, T-1, D]
        logits = self.fc(combined).squeeze(-1)  # [B, T-1]
        
        return logits


class AKT(nn.Module):
    """
    Attentive Knowledge Tracing (simplified).
    Uses monotonic attention with recency bias.
    """
    def __init__(self, num_skills, embed_dim=256, num_heads=8, num_layers=2, 
                 dropout=0.2, max_seq_len=512):
        super().__init__()
        self.num_skills = num_skills
        self.embed_dim = embed_dim
        self.num_heads = num_heads
        self.num_layers = num_layers
        
        # Knowledge embeddings (for key/value)
        self.interaction_embed = nn.Embedding(2 * num_skills + 1, embed_dim, padding_idx=2 * num_skills)
        # Exercise embeddings (for query)
        self.exercise_embed = nn.Embedding(num_skills + 1, embed_dim, padding_idx=num_skills)
        # Positional encoding
        self.pos_embed = nn.Embedding(max_seq_len + 2, embed_dim)
        
        # Attention layers
        self.attn_layers = nn.ModuleList([
            nn.MultiheadAttention(embed_dim, num_heads, dropout=dropout, batch_first=True)
            for _ in range(num_layers)
        ])
        self.ffn_layers = nn.ModuleList([
            nn.Sequential(
                nn.Linear(embed_dim, embed_dim * 4),
                nn.GELU(),
                nn.Dropout(dropout),
                nn.Linear(embed_dim * 4, embed_dim),
            )
            for _ in range(num_layers)
        ])
        self.norm1 = nn.ModuleList([nn.LayerNorm(embed_dim) for _ in range(num_layers)])
        self.norm2 = nn.ModuleList([nn.LayerNorm(embed_dim) for _ in range(num_layers)])
        self.norm_q = nn.ModuleList([nn.LayerNorm(embed_dim) for _ in range(num_layers)])
        
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout)
        self.fc = nn.Linear(embed_dim, 1)
        
    def forward(self, skill_ids, corrects, lengths):
        B, T = skill_ids.shape
        device = skill_ids.device
        T1 = T - 1
        
        # Key/Value: interaction history (positions 0..T-2)
        interaction_ids = skill_ids[:, :-1] + corrects[:, :-1] * self.num_skills
        positions = torch.arange(T1, device=device).unsqueeze(0)
        
        kv = self.interaction_embed(interaction_ids) + self.pos_embed(positions)
        kv = self.dropout(kv)
        
        # Query: exercise to predict (positions 1..T-1)
        q_positions = torch.arange(1, T, device=device).unsqueeze(0)
        q = self.exercise_embed(skill_ids[:, 1:]) + self.pos_embed(q_positions)
        q = self.dropout(q)
        
        # Causal mask: query at position i can attend to keys 0..i
        # attn_mask[i,j]=True means query i CANNOT attend to key j
        attn_mask = torch.triu(torch.ones(T1, T1, device=device), diagonal=1).bool()
        
        # Key padding mask
        key_padding_mask = torch.arange(T1, device=device).unsqueeze(0) >= (lengths.unsqueeze(1) - 1)
        
        for i in range(self.num_layers):
            # Pre-norm cross-attention
            q_norm = self.norm_q[i](q)
            kv_norm = self.norm1[i](kv)
            attn_out, _ = self.attn_layers[i](
                q_norm, kv_norm, kv_norm,
                attn_mask=attn_mask,
                key_padding_mask=key_padding_mask
            )
            q = q + attn_out
            # FFN
            q = q + self.ffn_layers[i](self.norm2[i](q))
        
        logits = self.fc(q).squeeze(-1)  # [B, T-1]
        return logits
